mnemonics.title: XAI Techniques
mnemonics.content:
LIME: Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations - explains individual predictions by approximating locally
SHAP: SHapley Additive exPlanations - uses game theory for fair feature attribution
Grad-CAM: Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping - visual explanations for CNNs

mnemonics.title: AI Ethics Principles
mnemonics.content:
F.T.A.P.H. - Fairness, Transparency, Accountability, Privacy, Human-centered design
Remember: 'Fair AI Takes Priority' - AI must be Fair, Transparent, Accountable, Prioritize Privacy, Human-centered

acronyms.usage: Techniques and methods that make AI decisions understandable to humans
Post-hoc explanation technique for individual predictions
Feature attribution method based on Shapley values from game theory
EU regulation giving individuals right to explanation for automated decisions
Sub-field of AI focusing on ethical AI systems

analogies.analogy: Like a mysterious chef who prepares excellent food but refuses to share recipes - you enjoy the result but have no idea how it was made
Like asking someone to describe a complex painting by showing them small sections and noting their reactions to each part
Like dividing credit for a team victory among all players based on their marginal contribution to winning
Like asking 'What would have happened if I had chosen option B instead of option A?' to understand decision logic
Like optical illusions for AI - images that look normal to humans but trick AI into seeing completely different things

visualTips.visualization: Think of a bar chart where each bar represents a feature's contribution to the prediction - taller bars = more influence on the decision
Imagine zooming in very close to a single point on a complex curved surface - locally it looks like a straight line that you can understand
Think of a tug-of-war: each feature pulls the prediction up or down, and SHAP shows exactly how much force each feature exerts
Imagine a map with colored regions - each color represents a different AI decision, and the boundaries show where the AI switches its prediction
